title: Convert Mixed Number to Improper Fraction
description: Convert a mixed number to an equivalent improper fraction before performing operations.
condition: A mixed number (e.g., 4 1/2 or 1 2/3) appears in a computation.
response: Rewrite the mixed number as an improper fraction using (whole × denominator + numerator)/denominator.
label: Principle
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Count Fractional Unit Cubes
description: Determine how many fractional edge-length cubes fit along a prism edge by dividing total length by cube edge length.
condition: Prism edge length L and unit cube edge length u are given.
response: Compute L ÷ u to find number of cubes along that edge.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Applied to each dimension before multiplying counts.

title: Determine if Quotient Is Less Than 1 by Comparing Fractions
description: Decide whether a fraction division result is less than 1 by comparing dividend and divisor.
condition: An expression a/b ÷ c/d where a/b and c/d are positive fractions.
response: Conclude quotient < 1 if dividend < divisor; > 1 if dividend > divisor.
label: Principle

title: Divide a Fraction by a Fraction
description: Compute the quotient of two fractions by multiplying by the reciprocal of the divisor.
condition: An expression of the form a/b ÷ c/d.
response: Rewrite as a/b × d/c and simplify.
label: Principle

title: Divide Whole Number by a Fraction
description: Dividing a number by a fraction a/b is equivalent to multiplying by b/a.
condition: Expression of the form n ÷ (a/b)
response: Rewrite as n × (b/a)
label: Principle

title: Find Prism Height from Volume and Base Area
description: Compute the height of a rectangular prism by dividing volume by base area.
condition: Volume V and base area B of a rectangular prism are given; height unknown.
response: Compute V ÷ B.
label: Principle / Model

title: Find Unknown Side from Area
description: Use division to find a missing side length of a rectangle when area and one side are known.
condition: Rectangle area A and one side length s are given; need the other side.
response: Compute A ÷ s.
label: Principle / Model
example: If $A = 5\tfrac{5}{8}$ and $s = 1\tfrac{1}{4}$, then width $= A \div s = 5\tfrac{5}{8} \div 1\tfrac{1}{4} = 4\tfrac{1}{2}$.

title: Interpret Division as “How Many Groups?”
description: Model a situation asking how many groups of a given size fit into a quantity using division.
condition: A word problem asks “How many groups?” or “How many batches/boxes?” given total and group size.
response: Represent with total ÷ group size.
label: Principle

title: Interpret Division as “How Much in One Group?”
description: Model a situation asking for the size of one group given total and number of groups.
condition: A word problem asks for amount per 1 unit given total for several units.
response: Represent with total ÷ number of units.
label: Principle

title: Interpret Division in Two Ways
description: Interpret a division expression as answering either a 'How many groups?' or a 'How many in each group?' question.
condition: A division expression of the form a ÷ b in an equal-groups context.
response: State both interpretations: (1) a items split into groups of size b, or (2) a items split into b equal groups.
label: Rule / Plan
example: For $28 \div 7$, this can mean: (1) 28 items in groups of 7 each, or (2) 28 items split into 7 equal groups.
notes: Classification as Rule / Plan assumes interpreting both forms is a taught convention rather than derived each time.???

title: Match Equivalent Equal-Group Representations
description: Determine whether a verbal description, equation, or diagram represents the same equal-groups relationship as a given multiplication equation.
condition: Given a multiplication equation (e.g., a × b = c) and alternative representations (verbal statements, diagrams, or division equations).
response: Select all representations that express the same relationship among the three quantities.
label: Principle / Model
example: Given $6 \times ? = 54$, matching representations include $54 \div 6 = ?$ and a diagram showing 6 equal parts totaling 54.

title: Multiplication Model of Equal Groups
description: Recognize that total equals number of groups multiplied by size of each group.
condition: An equal-size groups situation with a known relationship among total, number of groups, and size per group.
response: Represent the situation with (number of groups) × (size of one group) = total.
label: Principle / Model
example: If there are 7 pages with 4 photos each, then $7 \times 4 = 28$.

title: Reason About Fraction as Equal Parts of Whole
description: Use the idea that a fraction represents equal parts of a whole to scale up or down.
condition: A quantity representing a fraction of a whole is given (e.g., 8/5 kg is 2/3 of a batch).
response: Find the value of one fractional part, then scale to the whole.
label: Principle / Model
example: If $\frac{8}{5}$ kg is $\frac{2}{3}$ of a batch, then $\frac{1}{3}$ is $\frac{4}{5}$ kg, so 1 batch is $3 \times \frac{4}{5} = \frac{12}{5}$ kg.

title: Recognize Equivalent Division Expressions
description: Determine whether two numerical expressions are equivalent by rewriting division by a fraction as multiplication or by restructuring factors.
condition: Multiple expressions intended to equal the same division expression (e.g., 6 ÷ 3/2)
response: Select or produce expressions that evaluate to the same numerical value
label: Principle

title: Represent Fraction Situation with Diagram
description: Draw and label a visual model (e.g., tape diagram) to represent fractional parts and wholes.
condition: A word problem involving a fraction of a whole and a related quantity.
response: Partition a bar into equal fractional parts and label known and unknown quantities.
label: Production / Skill
example: Draw a bar split into 3 equal parts; label 2 parts as $\frac{8}{5}$ kg and all 3 parts as 1 batch.

title: Represent Situation with Multiplication Equation
description: Write a multiplication equation relating number of groups, size of group, and total.
condition: A groups-and-total context with one unknown value.
response: Write equation: (number of groups) × (size of group) = total.
label: Rule / Plan

title: Scale Fractional Part to Whole
description: Multiply the value of one fractional part by the number of equal parts in the whole.
condition: Value of 1/n of a whole is known and the whole (n/n) is required.
response: Multiply the 1/n value by n.
label: Principle / Model
example: If $\frac{1}{3}$ of a batch is $\frac{4}{5}$ kg, then 1 batch is $3 \times \frac{4}{5} = \frac{12}{5}$ kg.

title: Use Visual Model to Represent Fraction Division
description: Represent fraction division with a tape diagram or number line partitioned into equal fractional units.
condition: Prompt requests a diagram to justify fraction division reasoning.
response: Partition a whole into equal fractional parts and count how many divisor-sized parts fit in the dividend.
label: Production / Skill
example: Show $5/6$ as $10/12$ and count 10 segments of $1/12$.
notes: Non-verbal classification reflects reliance on diagram construction.

title: Volume of 1/2-Inch Cube
description: Recognize that a cube with edge length 1/2 inch has volume 1/8 cubic inch.
condition: Cube with edge length 1/2 unit.
response: State volume as (1/2)^3 = 1/8 cubic units.
label: Fact
notes: Specific to 1/2-edge cube in this task.

title: Write Related Multiplication Equation for Division
description: Express a division situation as an equivalent multiplication equation with an unknown factor.
condition: A question asks to write a multiplication equation for “How many a/b are in c/d?”
response: Write ? × a/b = c/d.
label: Principle
